creator: arved@FreeBSD.org
text:
-------- Original Message --------
Subject: Re: ports/156847: make of port www/py-html5lib does nothing
Date: Sat, 14 May 2011 15:41:55 +0100
From: Richard Brooksby <rb@ravenbrook.com>
To: arved@FreeBSD.org
CC: freebsd-ports-bugs@FreeBSD.org

raven# python -V
Python 2.7.1
raven# make clean
===>  Cleaning for py27-milter-0.8.7_1
raven# make
===>  License check disabled, port has not defined LICENSE
===>  Extracting for py27-milter-0.8.7_1
=> SHA256 Checksum OK for python/milter-0.8.7.tar.gz.
[etc.]

We have py-milter, py-gd, and other Python-based ports that built and 
installed correctly.

On 2011-05-14, at 13:47, arved@FreeBSD.org wrote:

> Synopsis: make of port www/py-html5lib does nothing
>
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: arved
> State-Changed-When: Sat May 14 12:46:05 UTC 2011
> State-Changed-Why:
> Can not reproduce. We need more details.
>
> Which python version do you have installed?
> Do other python ports work as expected?
>
>
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=156847
